title: [Bug] LiPo thermal runaway on P-1062 during hot-day endurance test
text:
Bug KES-208. Reporter: Aisha Mbeki (Test & Verification Lead). Assignee: Lena Okafor (Battery & Power). Status: Done. Priority: Critical. Created: 2023-09-08. Resolved: 2023-10-19.

Description: During the 8 September 2023 hot-day endurance test (ambient temperature 34°C, full payload, 75-minute mission profile), the P-1062 battery cell assembly (LiPo 6S2P) experienced a thermal runaway event at T+47 minutes. The BMS (P-1075) triggered the emergency disconnect at 82°C cell temperature, which is within the BMS protection range, but the cells had already begun venting. Post-event inspection confirmed one cell in the pack had vented and another showed measurable deformation. No fire occurred; the battery enclosure (P-1061) contained the event.

This is the second thermal anomaly on P-1062 cells — the first was a minor cell swelling event in March 2023 noted in the lab log but not escalated to a Jira issue.

Acceptance criteria for closure: Battery chemistry change ECR-214 approved and ECN-312 issued.

Linked to: ECR-214 (battery chemistry change), WIKI-052 (design review meeting), ECN-312 (approved change).
